assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_044",
  "rule_id": "Verify Permissions on /var/log/messages File",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "rationale": "Changing file permissions on a log file to 0600 is a standard STIG hardening step and does not affect system boot, authentication, or networking.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Potential loss of readability for non‑root users or processes that expect broader log access"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Run: sudo chmod 0600 /var/log/messages",
    "Verify the file ownership remains root:root",
    "Confirm logging continues without errors"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
